spec_match.rationale: The demo now starts two SpeechRecognition instances with two real MediaStreamTracks: the microphone track and a Web Audio MediaStreamDestination track that stands in for a remote participant track.

interactive.rationale: Start/stop drives real getUserMedia, Web Audio track creation, two recognizers, live track metadata, caption output, and lifecycle logs.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature index now describes one recognizer bound to the mic and a second recognizer bound to a Web Audio-routed MediaStreamTrack, matching the page.

edge_cases.rationale: Recognizer errors and track-overload fallback are surfaced in lane metadata and logs, but the page still does not provide a track-ending probe or scale beyond two tracks.

design_system.rationale: Variables only, two-tone pill states, no static-card pattern, mobile responsive grid.

openQuestions.title: Scale to N participants
openQuestions.detail: Two is a contrived case. Real conferences have 4-20 attendees. Add an `add participant` button that spawns a new lane + recognizer per click, tied to a synthesised audio source. The scaling cost (CPU? memory? quota?) is what authors actually want to know.

openQuestions.title: Handle track ending and recognizer error
openQuestions.detail: Add a `kick track #2` button that calls `track.stop()` and shows what happens to the recognizer (does `onend` fire? does it freeze?). Errors now surface in lane metadata, but the ended-track branch is still not directly exercisable.

summary: The fake second caption lane has been replaced with a real Web Audio-derived MediaStreamTrack and a second SpeechRecognition instance. Remaining work is to scale the pattern and add an explicit ended-track probe.
